rulings.Case Title: MINDY FREILE VS. TIM LINCECUM et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Reconsideration Of The Court'S July 26, 2013, Discovery Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 27, 2013, Line 3: Plaintiff MINDY FREILE'S Motion For Reconsideration Of The Court's July 26, 2013, Discovery Order Pro Tem Judge James Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. The moving party failed to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. The moving party shall e-mail a copy of the moving papers and proof of service to jamesw@skwsf.com as soon as possible AND provide a copy of its moving papers at the hearing on August 27, 2013, by 8:45 a.m. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). If the papers are not provided, the hearing will be taken off calendar. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JOESIAH BELL VS. 24 HOUR FITNESS USA, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Compel Discovery Responses , Production Of Documents And Depositions; Memo Of P&A; Proof Of Service
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, August 27, 2013, Line 4, PLAINTIFF JOESIAH BELL'S Motion To Compel Discovery Responses, Production Of Documents And Depositions Granted as to Special Interrogatories nos. 5, 7, 11, 12, 13, 15, 16, 17, 18, Requests for Production Nos. 2, 6, 9, and PMK depositions I, II and III. Denied as to Special Interrogatory no. 6. Mass Movement's responses to Requests for Production 2, 6, and 9, and its responses (both initial and supplemental) to all of Plaintiff's interrogatories with the exception of Interrogatory 6, are deficient. Plaintiff's discovery is aimed at collecting relevant evidence that is either admissible or reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. Within 15 days, Mass Movement shall provide verified further responses to Special Interrogatories Nos. 5, 7, 11, 12, 13, 15, 16, 17, 18, Requests Request for Production nos. 2, 6 and 9, and Mass Movement shall produce all documents in its possession, custody, or control responsive to Request for Production numbers 2, 6 and 9. Within 30 days, Mass Movement shall designate and produce for deposition at Tooker & Antz, 350 Sansome Street, Suite 700, San Francisco, CA 94104 those of its officers, directors, managing agents, employees, or agents who are most qualified to testify on its behalf, to the extent of any information known or reasonably available to Mass Movement, about those matters identified in Plaintiff's Notices of Deposition of Defendant Mass Movement, Inc.'s Person Most Knowledgeable I, II, and III. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the specific portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: KEVIN LINDSETH VS. BOLIGALA RAJU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Renewed Motion To Dismiss Or Stay Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, August 27, 2013, Line 10, DEFENDANT HIGH QUALITY ORGANICS, INC.'S Notice Of Motion And Renewed Motion To Dismiss Or Stay Proceedings Denied. Moving party has not shown a basis to grant this renewed motion to dismiss or stay proceedings. Moving party has not shown that the balace of private and public interest factors shows justice would be served if the litigation proceeded in an alternative forum. Trial is scheduled to begin in San Francisco on September 16, 2013. rulings.Case Title: ERICA SKONE-REES VS. WEST ELM et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Quash Pltff'S Deposition Subpoena Of Insurance Adjuster Jeffrey A. Roger;
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, August 27, 2013, Line 13, DEFENDANT WILLIAMS-SONOMA, INC, WEST ELM DBA WILLIAMS-SONOMA STORES, INC.'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Plaintiff's Deposition Subpoena Of Insurance Adjuster Jeffrey A. Roger Denied without prejudice to making objections at the deposition or at trial. Depositions can be taken of non-percipient witnesses. Defendant has not established that the deposition falls outside the scope of CCP §2017.010 or that it necessarily calls for communications covered by the attorney client privilege. The distance from Concord to San Francisco is not a basis to grant a protective order. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: TERRENCE BARNUM VS. PAUL RYAN ASSOCIATES INC., et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Provide Discovery Responses And Produce Documents, And For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff And His Counsel
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 27, 2013, Line 7: Defendants PETER HEELAN'S and PAUL RYAN ASSOCIATES, INC.'S Motion to Compel Plaintiff to Provide Discovery Responses and Produce Documents, and for Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff and His Counsel Pro Tem Judge James Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Grant the motion. The issue of sanctions is reserved for the hearing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jamesw@skwsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: STEVEN KRYSTOFIAK VS. JOHN KITHAS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Leave To File First Amended Cross-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, August 27, 2013, Line 16, DEFENDANT JOHN KITHAS' Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Cross-Complaint Denied without prejudice. The motion is premature. This case was ordered to arbitration pursuant to CCP §1141.11(c). An arbitration award was filed on July 17, 2013. The arbitrator's award will become final "unless a request for a de novo trial or a request for dismissal in the form required by the Judicial Council is filed within 60 days after the date the arbitrator files the award with the court." CCP §1141.20. To date, no party has challenged the arbitrator's award by requesting a trial de novo. Unless and until that occurs, the motion for leave to file first amended cross-complaint is premature. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: REAL ESTATE EBROKER, INC. VS. EDWARD LITKE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Andm Omtion To Compel Response To Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatires, And Toproduce Documents,
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 27, 2013, Line 8: Defendant EDWARD LITKE'S Motion to Compel Response to Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, and to Produce Documents Pro Tem Judge James Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. The parties shall e-mail a copy of all papers filed in support of and in opposition to the motion to jamesw@skwsf.com as soon as possible AND provide a copy of these papers at the hearing on August 27, 2013, by 8:45 a.m. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JERRY RODRIGUEZ VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, August 27, 2013, Line 22, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Judge Miller discloses and will discloses at the hearing that she knows and is social friends with Deputy City Attorney Elizabeth Salveson. Judge Miller practiced law in the same firm as Ms Salveson from December 1981 - March 1987, and from 1992 through 1995. This will not affect Judge Miller's ability to be fair and impartial in this case, and she does not believe that a person aware of these facts would reasonably have a doubt that she would be able to be impartial. Sustained, with ten days leave to amend to state a cause of action against defendant. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
